problem.title: 3Sum
problem.description: Given an integer array nums, return all the triplets [nums[i], nums[j], nums[k]] such that i != j, i != k, and j != k, and nums[i] + nums[j] + nums[k] == 0.
assumptions: The solution set must not contain duplicate triplets.

example.explanation: The two triplets that sum to zero are [-1,-1,2] and [-1,0,1].

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Two Pointers
solutions.description: Sort the array so that for each fixed element we can find a pair summing to its negation using two pointers converging from both ends. Duplicate values are skipped at each pointer position to avoid repeating the same triplet in the output. The key insight is that sorting lets us deterministically shrink the search space in O(n) per outer element.
